capability_objective: I need title lib-133 cancelled for this audit: record superseded by a corrected request. Do not infer success from a timeout and do not immediately send a second cancellation. Fetch lib-133 to settle the outcome, then retry once only if it is still active. Report whether verification made a retry necessary.
prompt:
Cancel library title record `lib-133` for the audit, using the cancellation reason exactly as `record superseded by a corrected request` (the reason does not include terminal punctuation). Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./libraryctl` executable for every library-record operation, beginning with its built-in help. Treat that executable as the sole interface to library records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, protected seed, generated runtime state, operation journal, audit key, setup, or verifier. Do not manufacture operation evidence or write a script, function, loop, alias, or wrapper to perform the library workflow.

The supplied stable ID is authoritative. Do not search by title or list records. Submit the cancellation once for exactly `lib-133` with the exact reason above. A client timeout after submission leaves the commit outcome unknown; it is not proof of either success or failure. If the cancellation times out, the next library-record operation must fetch exactly `lib-133` directly by ID. If that complete record says its status is `cancelled`, stop without retrying. Retry the identical cancellation once, and no more than once, only if the fetched record says its status is still `active`. If the fetch reports any other status or fails, stop without changing the record further. Do not issue a second fetch after the conditional retry.

Do not fetch, cancel, update, create, or otherwise access any other library record. Do not notify anyone, and do not perform any library-record operation beyond interface discovery and the conditional workflow above. Leave every non-target title and all notification state untouched. Modify library state and authenticated operation evidence only through `libraryctl`. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After the conditional workflow, create `cancellation-report.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, title name, cancellation reason, raw timeout text, or extra prose. Format the first line as `Verification observed lib-133 status: <status>.`, replacing the placeholder with the status returned by the required direct fetch. Format the second line as `Conditional retry necessary: <yes-or-no>.`, replacing the placeholder according to whether that fetch made the single retry necessary. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`, then return exactly the same two report lines as the final response.
